rationale_source: R11-A inferential canonical field × broker_correction × BUG-7 correction-from-awaiting_collateral routing × BUG-4 canonical-incompleteness guard

transaction_type.rationale: F1.TT broker_correction wins per R11-A inferential field hierarchy. BUG-7 (BATCH 15): the correction now ROUTES correctly from awaiting_collateral (parseBrokerCorrections detects transaction_type=refinance → routed to active-branch R10-G), instead of being mis-classified as a collateral disposition.

mortgage_statement_now_required.rationale: ASSERTION-2: After TT correction to refinance, mortgage_statement requirement activates (was N/A for purchase per E06).

bug4_canonical_incompleteness_reescalate.rationale: BUG-4: property value is null from ALL sources (event[0] purchase + event[1] correction both omit it). The refi correction does not resolve the missing gate input, so the high-LTV escalation correctly re-fires (LLM ltv 89.6% > 80%).

workflow_state.rationale: SPEC REALIGNMENT (BATCH 15, BUG-7): the original spec 'active' was optimistic — it assumed the purchase→refi correction resolves everything. Reality: Bug-7 routes the correction correctly (transaction_type→refinance via R10-G), but the deal then re-escalates because Bug-4's canonical-incompleteness guard correctly fires on still-null property value (89.6% standalone, no value to recompute). Corrections that don't resolve the underlying gate inputs correctly re-trigger the gate — Bug-7 + Bug-4 composing as designed. Verified via staging logs (BUG-7 pre-check fires: 'correction-intent in awaiting_collateral (transaction_type=refinance) — routing to active-branch').

must_include.rationale: Re-escalation collateral-ask (89.6% high LTV, value still missing). FOLLOWUP: a correction-acknowledgment outbound is a documented UX gap (POST-CLOSURE-FOLLOWUP-ITEMS.md).
